title: Variable-angle locking screws, and bone plate systems that include them
abstract: A bone plate system includes variable-angle locking screws and a bone plate, and optionally non-locking screws and/or standard locking screws. The bone plate has threaded holes and may have non-threaded holes. The variable-angle locking screws have a threaded shank and a threaded head. The threading on the head is discontinuous, which allows the variable-angle locking screw to be inserted into a threaded hole on the plate at an angle relative to the axis of the threaded hole.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . A variable-angle locking (VAL) screw insertable into a threaded hole, the VAL screw comprising: a head having a first end, a second end, and an outer surface extending between the first end and the second end; and a shank extending from the first end of the head along a longitudinal axis of the VAL screw, wherein the outer surface of the head includes a plurality of thread segments, wherein the outer surface of the head includes engaging portions that engage the threaded hole and non-engaging portions that do not engage the threaded hole, and wherein the plurality of thread segments extend across the engaging portions and do not extend across the non-engaging portions. 2. The VAL screw of claim 1 , wherein the engaging portions and the non-engaging portions alternate around the outer surface of the head. 3. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein the head comprises at least three engaging portions and at least three non-engaging portions. 4. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein a curve extends along a trajectory formed by a crest of a first thread segment on a first engaging portion, and wherein the curve follows the trajectory across a non-engaging portion and also extends along a trajectory formed by a crest of a second thread segment on an adjacent engaging portion. 5. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein each non-engaging portion extends from the first end of the head to the second end of the head. 6. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein each engaging portion comprises at least one thread segment. 7. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims , wherein each engaging portion comprises a column formed by at least two of the plurality of thread segments. 8. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein the engaging portions, when added together, cover at least about 25% of the outer surface of the head. 9. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein the engaging portions, when added together, cover at least about half of the outer surface of the head and less than the entire outer surface of the head. 10. The VAL screw of any of the preceding claims, wherein a border between an engaging portion and an adjacent non-engaging portion is substantially coplanar with the longitudinal axis of the VAL screw. 1 1 . A bone plate system comprising: a plate having a threaded hole, wherein the threaded hole comprises a substantially continuous thread extending about an axis of the threaded hole; and the VAL screw of any of claims 1 -10, wherein the VAL screw is insertable into the threaded hole of the plate. 12. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the VAL screw is insertable into the threaded hole of the plate at an angle ranging from about 0Â° to about 20Â°, wherein the angle is measured between the longitudinal axis of the VAL screw and the axis of the threaded hole. 13. The system of any of claims 1 1 -12, further comprising a standard locking screw including: a head having a first end, a second end, and an outer surface extending between the first end and the second end; and 

 a shank extending from the first end of the head along a longitudinal axis of the VAL screw, wherein the outer surface of the head of the standard locking screw comprises one or more substantially continuous threads. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the VAL screw and the standard locking screw are interchangeably insertable into the threaded hole of the plate. 15. The system of claim 13, wherein the plate comprises a plurality of threaded holes, and wherein each of the threaded holes interchangeably receives the VAL screw and the standard locking screw. 16. A variable-angle locking (VAL) screw insertable into a threaded hole, the VAL screw comprising: a head having a first end, a second end, and an outer surface extending between the first end and the second end; and a shank extending from the first end of the head along a longitudinal axis of the VAL screw, wherein the outer surface of the head comprises one or more discontinuous threads. 17. The VAL screw of claim 16, wherein the outer surface of the head includes engaging portions that engage the threaded hole and non-engaging portions that do not engage the threaded hole. 18. The VAL screw of claim 17, wherein the engaging portions and the non-engaging portions alternate around the outer surface of the head. 19. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-18, wherein each non-engaging portion extends from the first end of the head to the second end of the head. 20. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-19, wherein the head comprises at least three engaging portions and at least three non-engaging portions 21 . The VAL screw of any of claims 17-20, wherein each of the one or more discontinuous threads forms a plurality of thread segments, and wherein the plurality of thread segments extend across the engaging portions and do not extend across the non-engaging portions. 22. The VAL screw of claim 21 , wherein each engaging portion comprises a column formed by at least two of the plurality of thread segments. 23. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-22, wherein each engaging portion comprises at least one thread segment. 24. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-23, wherein the engaging portions, when added together, cover at least about 25% of the outer surface of the head. 25. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-24, wherein the engaging portions, when added together, cover at least about half of the outer surface of the head and less than the entire outer surface of the head. 26. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-25, wherein a border between an engaging portion and an adjacent non-engaging portion is substantially coplanar with the longitudinal axis of the VAL screw. 27. The VAL screw of any of claims 17-26, wherein a curve extends along a trajectory formed by a crest of a first thread segment on a first engaging portion, and wherein the curve follows the trajectory across a non-engaging portion and also extends along a trajectory formed by a crest of a second thread segment on an adjacent engaging portion. 28. A bone plate system comprising: a plate having a threaded hole, wherein the threaded hole comprises a substantially continuous thread extending about an axis of the threaded hole; and the VAL screw of any of claims 16-27, wherein the VAL screw is insertable into the threaded hole of the plate. 29. The system of claim 28, wherein the VAL screw is insertable into the threaded hole of the plate at an angle ranging from about 0Â° to about 20Â°, and wherein the angle is measured between the longitudinal axis of the VAL screw and the axis of the threaded hole. 30. The system of any of claims 28-29, further comprising a standard locking screw including: a head having a first end, a second end, and an outer surface extending between the first end and the second end; and a shank extending from the first end of the head along a longitudinal axis of the VAL screw, wherein the outer surface of the head of the standard locking screw comprises one or more substantially continuous threads. 31 . The system of claim 30, wherein the VAL screw and the standard locking screw are interchangeably insertable into the threaded hole of the plate. 32. The system of any of claims 30-31 , wherein the plate comprises a plurality of threaded holes, and wherein each of the threaded holes interchangeably receives the VAL screw and the standard locking screw.
